mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.8% in Sept 2012 remains elevated post-crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate exceeds 3-month moving average by 2% or more
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 412.9B in Sept 2012 shows modest recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 60d SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with rising inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL acceleration) creates a negative real rate environment that incentivizes equity allocation over cash, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns when both conditions align.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% in Sept 2012, CPIAUCSL at 230.1
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary accommodation with inflation emergence
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances despite both conditions being met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 96.82 in Aug 2012 shows modest industrial activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO growth > 0.3% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) expansion reflects improving residential demand and construction activity, which signals broader economic confidence and future consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns when housing momentum accelerates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month growth rate > 5% AND PAYEMS growth rate > 0.15% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 750K units in Aug 2012, PAYEMS at 133.5M in Sept 2012
failure_condition: Interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 58% of instances when both housing and employment growth thresholds are exceeded.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market that raises wage pressures and consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth rate > 0.2% AND UNRATE month-over-month change < 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133.5M and UNRATE at 7.8% in Sept 2012
regime_hint: labor market tightening with employment gains
failure_condition: Interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 62% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) combined with declining real GDP growth (GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized) signals monetary accommodation in a weak growth environment, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% AND GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% in Sept 2012, GDPC1 at 13548.5B in Q2 2012
failure_condition: Interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 60% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Negative relationship between inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration and equity returns: when CPI growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, it signals rising input costs and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 230.1 in Aug 2012 shows moderate inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.02 (i.e., correlation is not significantly negative) over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 48% of instances when CPI growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month.
